steps.title: Spot the core tension
steps.content: ”It may be tempting to speculate” but “each novel is unique, resulting from a specific set of circumstances.” This contrast explains why the temptation is problematic: we cannot know what would happen if a novel were written differently.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D expresses the logical consequence: “there is no way to reasonably evaluate how a work would be different if it had been written by other means.” Since each novel is unique, we cannot assess how an alternative method would have changed it.

The other three propose an unsupported change (A), make comparative claims (B), or speculate about differences (C)—all contrary to the passage's point.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
